evidence_sentence: Nanog binds directly to Esrrb, enhances binding of RNAPolII, and stimulates Esrrb transcription.
cell_type_sentences: Embryonic stem cell (ESC) self-renewal efficiency is determined by the level of Nanog expression.
Here we investigate ESCs expressing different Nanog levels and Nanog(-/-) cells with distinct functionally inducible Nanog proteins to identify Nanog-responsive genes.
Overexpression of Esrrb in ESCs maintains cytokine-independent self-renewal and pluripotency.
Remarkably, this activity is retained in Nanog(-/-) ESCs.
Moreover, Esrrb can reprogram Nanog(-/-) EpiSCs and can rescue stalled reprogramming in Nanog(-/-) pre-iPSCs.
Finally, Esrrb deletion abolishes the defining ability of Nanog to confer LIF-independent ESC self-renewal.

evidence_sentence: Both ESRRB and NANOG are necessary for maintaining the activity of this promoter in ES cell lines.
cell_type_sentences: Esrrb activates Oct4 transcription and sustains self-renewal and pluripotency in embryonic stem cells.

Recent studies have shown that knockdown of Esrrb (estrogen-related-receptor beta), a member of the nuclear orphan receptor family, induces differentiation of mouse ES cells cultured in the presence of leukemia inhibitory factor.
In summary, our experiments help to understand how Esrrb coordinates with Nanog and Oct4 to activate the internal machinery of ES cells.
Integration of external signaling pathways with the core transcriptional network in embryonic stem cells.

evidence_sentence: The second group (Oct4, Nanog, Sox2, Smad1, Stat3, Tcfcp2l1, and Esrrb) may serve as either activator or repressor depending on the target.
cell_type_sentences: ChIP-Seq of transcription factors predicts absolute and differential gene expression in embryonic stem cells.
In mouse embryonic stem cells (ESCs), we find that a remarkably high proportion of variation in gene expression (65%) can be explained by the binding signals of 12 transcription factors (TFs).
The two groups of TFs cooperate tightly to activate genes that are differentially up-regulated in ESCs.
In the absence of binding by the first group, the binding of the second group is associated with genes that are repressed in ESCs and derepressed upon early differentiation.

evidence_sentence: Esrrb binds to silenced enhancers containing stable nucleosomes and hypermethylated DNA, which are inaccessible to the core factors.
We show that Tcf3 acts broadly on a genome-wide scale to reduce the levels of several promoters of self-renewal (Nanog, Tcl1, Tbx3, Esrrb) while not affecting other ESC genes.
cell_type_sentences: Tcf3 functions as a steady-state limiter of transcriptional programs of mouse embryonic stem cell self-renewal.
Three transcription factors, Nanog, Oct4, and Sox2, have been reported to form a feedforward circuit promoting pluripotent cell self-renewal in embryonic stem cells (ESC).
Previously, we found that a transcriptional repressor activity of Tcf3, a DNA-binding effector of Wnt signaling, reduced Nanog promoter activity and Nanog levels in mouse embryonic stem cells (mESC).
We show that Tcf3 acts broadly on a genome-wide scale to reduce the levels of several promoters of self-renewal (Nanog, Tcl1, Tbx3, Esrrb) while not affecting other ESC genes (Oct4, Sox2, Fgf4).
Interestingly, the effects of Tcf3 were more strongly correlated with Oct4 than with Nanog, despite the normal levels of Oct4 in TCF3-/- mESC.
The deranged gene expression allowed TCF3-/- mESC self-renewal even in the absence of leukemia inhibitory factor and delayed differentiation in embryoid bodies.

evidence_sentence: Through chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) sequencing and microarray experiments, we further demonstrate that Ncoa3 shares overlapping gene regulatory functions with Esrrb and cooperates genome-wide with the Oct4-Sox2-Nanog circuitry at active enhancers to up-regulate genes involved in self-renewal and pluripotency.
cell_type_sentences: Ncoa3 functions as an essential Esrrb coactivator to sustain embryonic stem cell self-renewal and reprogramming.
Embryonic stem cell (ESC) pluripotency depends on a well-characterized gene regulatory network centered on Oct4, Sox2, and Nanog.
In contrast, little is known about the identity of the key coregulators and the mechanisms by which they may potentiate transcription in ESCs.

Alongside core transcription factors, the orphan nuclear receptor Esrrb (estrogen-related receptor β) is vital for the maintenance of ESC identity and furthermore is uniquely associated with the basal transcription machinery.
Here, we show that Ncoa3, an essential coactivator, is required to mediate Esrrb function in ESCs.
We propose an integrated model of transcriptional and coactivator control, mediated by Ncoa3, for the maintenance of ESC self-renewal and somatic cell reprogramming.

evidence_sentence: Co-expression of NANOG and TET1 increases 5-hydroxymethylcytosine levels at the top-ranked common target loci Esrrb and Oct4 (also called Pou5f1), resulting in priming of their expression before reprogramming to naive pluripotency.
cell_type_sentences: Using enhanced purification techniques and a stringent computational algorithm, we identify 27 high-confidence protein interaction partners of NANOG in mouse embryonic stem cells.
TET1 and NANOG co-occupy genomic loci of genes associated with both maintenance of pluripotency and lineage commitment in embryonic stem cells, and TET1 binding is reduced upon NANOG depletion.
